data.title: Record-decision trigger taxonomy: what counts as a recordable decision
data.claim: Use /knowledge-distillery:record-decision for scope decisions, architectural choices, confirmed constraints, and direction-after-deliberation — not for user preferences, temporary debugging choices, or implementation details obvious from code.
data.body:
## Background
The record-decision skill was introduced because project decisions made during sessions were only captured in auto-memory and never reached the distillery pipeline. To prevent both over-triggering (recording noise) and under-triggering (missing real decisions), the PR established an explicit taxonomy of what qualifies as a recordable decision.

## Details
Trigger record-decision for: scope decisions ("X is out of scope"), architectural choices ("use approach A for Z"), confirmed constraints ("we can't do X because Y"), direction confirmed after deliberating alternatives.

Do NOT trigger for: user preferences such as editor settings, communication style, or workflow habits (use auto-memory instead); temporary choices like debugging workarounds or local-only configuration; implementation details that are obvious from reading the code (naming a variable, choosing a loop construct); situations where the user is still deliberating and has not committed to a direction.

## Rejected Alternatives
Not applicable — this is a taxonomy fact, not an anti-pattern.

## Open Questions
The boundary between "architectural choice" and "implementation detail obvious from code" may require judgment in edge cases. The current guidance relies on agent discretion.

## Stop Conditions
Revisit if the false-positive rate (too many trivial decisions recorded) or false-negative rate (missed decisions) becomes problematic in practice.
data.considerations: This taxonomy was established for a solo-developer project where the owner is the entire team. In multi-person teams, the bar for what constitutes an explicit team decision may be higher and additional review signals may be required before triggering.

data.title: PR body template enforcement is out of scope for knowledge-distillery
data.claim: Do not provide PR body templates or format guidelines as part of knowledge-distillery — rely on pipeline prompt improvements if extraction accuracy needs to increase.
data.body:
## Background
The question arose whether knowledge-distillery should provide a PR description template to improve the quality of evidence available to extract-candidates. The team reviewed whether structured PR body sections were necessary for the pipeline to function.

## Details
The extract-candidates stage is LLM-based and can extract signal from free-form PR descriptions without requiring structured sections. Because every project has its own PR body conventions, knowledge-distillery imposing a specific format would be inappropriate. If extraction accuracy becomes insufficient, the correct remedy is to improve the pipeline-side prompts, not to mandate a PR format.

This also keeps the tool vendor-neutral and non-prescriptive about project conventions.

## Rejected Alternatives
Considered adding a PR description template to the repository or documenting a recommended PR body format. Rejected because LLM-based extraction already handles free-form descriptions adequately.

## Open Questions
None identified. If extraction accuracy degrades significantly with unusual PR formats, revisit whether lightweight guidance (not enforcement) is warranted.

## Stop Conditions
Revisit if the pipeline extraction quality drops noticeably on real-world PRs that lack any descriptive body text.
data.considerations: This decision was made in the context of a project that dogfoods its own pipeline. The conclusion that free-form PR bodies are sufficient may need re-evaluation if knowledge-distillery is applied to projects with very terse or low-quality PR descriptions.

data.title: Archive rejected vault entries rather than deleting
data.claim: MUST archive rejected vault entries using _pipeline-archive rather than deleting them, to preserve history.
data.body:
## Background
When the /curate workflow processes reviewer feedback on a Report PR, rejected entries must not be permanently removed. The PR body explicitly states: "Rejected entries are archived in vault.db (preserves history, not deleted)". The `_pipeline-archive` CLI command was introduced specifically to set `status='archived'` with a timestamp and reason while leaving the row intact.

## Details
The `pipeline_archive` function sets `status='archived'`, `archived_at=datetime('now')`, and `archive_reason` on the entry row — it does NOT execute a DELETE. The `_pipeline-archive` command validates that the entry exists and is not already archived before proceeding. When querying for active knowledge, all queries filter on `archived_at IS NULL` or `status='active'`, so archived entries are invisible to agents but recoverable for audit.

## Rejected Alternatives
Deleting rows was rejected in favor of archiving. Deletion would make audit history impossible and prevent understanding why certain entries were removed. The append-only approach allows the pipeline to track the full lifecycle of each entry.

## Stop Conditions
Reconsider if vault storage becomes a concern at scale and a separate archive table is introduced.
data.considerations: The archive-only approach means the vault grows monotonically. Re-running curation on an already-archived entry is a no-op (CLI returns 'already archived'). This is a deliberate append-only design choice consistent with the project's philosophy of preserving evidence trails.

entries.reject_reason: 구현 디테일 수준으로 vault에 넣을 만큼의 가치가 없음

data.title: Decision commits do not require a memento note
data.claim: Do not attach a memento git note after a decision: commit — the decision file itself serves as the session context.
data.considerations: This exception applies specifically to decision: commits.

entries.reject_reason: 워크플로우 트리거 조건은 코드에서 자명한 구현 디테일

data.title: Restrict /curate trigger to knowledge/batch-* branches only
data.claim: MUST validate that the target PR branch starts with 'knowledge/batch-' before executing any curation actions.
data.considerations: The branch name format is the established convention.

entries.reject_reason: 컨벤션에 따른 당연한 동작으로 별도 지식으로 관리할 필요 없음

data.title: The /curate slash command trigger is intentionally case-sensitive
data.claim: MUST use lowercase '/curate' exactly to trigger curation.
data.considerations: This is an explicit convention alignment decision.
